name: Dazzling Light
effect:
You create a small point of light at the target location. The light provides dim light in short range of it. The light can also move with you while staying in range. When targeted at a creature, you can make the light violently explode instead.
Weak. Deal +2 radiant damage.
Strong. Deal +4 radiant damage. The target is also briefly blinded.
Critical. Deal +6 radiant damage. The target is also briefly blinded and dazed.

name: Detect Lies
effect:
You focus your senses on all creatures in range, gaining a glimpse into their immediate emotions.
For any creature affected by this spell, you gain the following effects:
- They suffer +1 bane on attacks against you.
- The creature is never considered hidden from you, even if they are invisible.
- You gain +1 boon on Insight rolls to determine their emotions, if they are lying, or concealing something.
This spell lasts for a short duration or until an affected creature moves out of this spell’s range.

name: Illuminated Sight
effect:
You concentrate light energy into your eyes, enhancing your eye sight and making you able to pierce through concealing environments.
On a success, you gain the following effects:
- You can see one range category further from sources of bright and dim light.
- You gain +1 boon on Perception rolls.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Revealing Burst
effect:
You let loose a quick burst of radiant energy in all directions, revealing any magical falsehoods, such as illusions, shapeshifters, or invisible creatures in range. For illusions, compare your result vs. the Resist of the illusion’s creator to see if they are broken.
Weak. Any magical falsehood is marked by a shimmer of light. Rolls that target any marked target gain +1 boon. The mark lasts for a short duration.
Strong. Any magical falsehood is immediately broken.
Critical. Any magical falsehood is immediately broken and any creature revealed by this is briefly dazed.

name: Blessing of Light
effect:
You bless a creature with the magical power of light, making them enlightened to their surroundings.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You can see one range category further from sources of bright and dim light.
- You gain +1 boon on Perception rolls.
This spell lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target gains resistance to fire and radiant damage for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target gains resistance to fire, necrotic, psychic, and radiant damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Locate Trinket
effect:
Touch a small object and imbue it with truth revealing magic. The object must comfortably fit within the palm of your hand and can be no larger.
On a success, you always know the location of the imbued trinket while it is in the same sphere of existence as you. This spell lasts for a long duration.

name: Protect from Influence
effect:
Channel the light’s protective magic into one creature within range, bolstering their mental defenses. Cast this spell as a Quick Action on your turn.
On a success, the target gains +1 boon on rolls to resist any mental effects and gains resistance to psychic damage. Attempts to influence their mind also suffer +1 bane. This spell lasts for a short duration.

name: Radiant Burst
effect:
You let forth a burst of radiant energy, streaking towards a target location and exploding in burning light.
Weak. Deal +2 radiant damage to any creature in melee range of the target location.
Strong. Deal +4 radiant damage to any creature in melee range of the target location. Each target is also briefly blinded.
Critical. Deal +6 radiant damage to any creature in melee range of the target location. Each target is also briefly blinded and dazed.

name: Radiant Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of the sun. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon emits bright light in melee range and dim light in close range of it and deals +2 radiant damage. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon emits bright light in close range and dim light in short range and deals +4 radiant damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon emits bright light in short range and dim light in medium range and deals +6 radiant damage instead.

name: Sense Spirits
effect:
You open your inner eye to the sacred and the profane, and the presence of the otherworldly burns clear before you. You sense the presence of Spirit and Undead creatures within long range for a medium duration, even through obstacles, and whether any ground within range has been consecrated or desecrated. While the spell lasts, you perceive such creatures even while they are ethereal, invisible, or otherwise hidden, and you can target them normally.
Weak. You learn the number of such creatures and the rough direction of each.
Strong. You also learn each one's precise location and its kind, such as a celestial, an infernal spirit, or a restless ghost.
Critical. As Strong, and for each you sense its disposition toward you, one of bound, hostile, or at rest, and a rough sense of its current purpose or command, whichever the GM finds fitting, such as guarding, hunting, waiting, or serving a master's order. If nothing concrete fits, you catch only a wordless impression of its mood.

name: Sun Sphere
effect:
You conjure a small orb of flame resembling a miniature sun.
On a success, the sphere appears at the target location. It also shines bright light in close range and dim light in short range of it. Any creature in melee range of it takes +4 fire damage when they first come into contact with it and at the start of each of their turns.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to move the beam a close distance within range. The sphere lasts for a short duration.

name: True Strike
effect:
You focus on a creatures thoughts, gaining insight into their defenses. Cast this spell on your turn.
On a success, increase the SL of your next attack until the end of your next turn by one step (max. critical success).

name: Break Curse
effect:
You initiate a purifying ritual to remove harmful influences from a creature’s soul.
On a success, you break one curse the creature is afflicted with. Any single creature can only benefit from this spell once per day.

name: Destroy Undeath
effect:
You channel the power of divine light, flooding undead creatures around you with it in an attempt to annihilate them instantly.
Weak. Any undead creature in range of tier 0 or 1 is instantly destroyed and can’t reanimate.
Strong. Any undead creature in range of tier 0 or 1 is instantly destroyed and can’t reanimate. Any other undead creature in range is also briefly frightened of you, ignoring any immunity they might have against the condition.
Critical. Any undead creature in range of tier 0 or 1 is instantly destroyed and can’t reanimate. Any other undead creature in range is also frightened of you for a short duration, ignoring any immunity they might have against the condition. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Sunbeam
effect:
You call down a pillar of light at the target location, revealing any falsehoods and burning creatures within it.
On a success, the beam extends in melee range of the target location and a medium distance in height. It also shines bright light in close range and dim light in short range of it. Any magical falsehoods, such as illusions, shapeshifters, or invisible creatures inside the beam is immediately revealed.
Any creatures inside the beam take +8 radiant damage when they first come into contact with it and at the start of each of their turns.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to move the beam a short distance within range. The beam lasts for a short duration.

name: Blessing of Dawn
effect:
You invoke the power of the rising sun, blessing an ally with dawn's radiance and renewal. On a success, the target gains the following effects for a short duration:
- They regain 4 HP at the start of each of their turns
- They gain immunity to fear and charm effects
- They can see through magical darkness and illusions
- Once per turn, they can re-roll any test
- They emit bright light in close range and dim light in short range

name: Purifying Light
effect:
You unleash a devastating cone of solar energy that burns and blinds everything in its path.
Weak. Deal +4 radiant damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 radiant damage to each target.
Critical. Deal +12 radiant damage to each target and they are briefly stunned.
Also on a success, each target is briefly blinded.

name: Solar Flare
effect:
You create an explosion of pure, cleansing light that burns away darkness and evil influences. Choose a close area within range. All creatures within the area are affected.
Weak. Deal +4 radiant damage to hostile creatures, restore +3 HP to allies, and remove one negative condition from all allies.
Strong. Deal +8 radiant damage to hostile creatures, restore +6 HP to allies, and remove up to two negative conditions from all allies.
Critical. Deal +12 radiant damage to hostile creatures, restore +9 HP to allies, and remove up to three negative conditions from all allies.
Any areas of magical darkness within range are permanently dispelled.

name: Dark Sight
effect:
You magically adjust your senses to adapt to total darkness, allowing you to see without any source of light.
On a success, you can see a medium distance in absolute darkness as in dim light. If you are exposed to bright light while under the effects of these spells, roll Spirit + Fortitude. On a failure, this spell ends and you are briefly dazed. Otherwise, this spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Night’s Grasp
effect:
You magically animate the shadows around a creature, clawing and grasping for the target with ghostly coldness.
Weak. The target suffers +2 frost damage.
Strong. The target suffers +4 frost damage and is briefly slowed.
Critical. The target suffers +6 frost damage and is briefly grappled by the shadows.
You can also cast this spell to cool down an object, such as extinguishing the last embers of a campfire.

name: Obscuring Veil
effect:
You weave shadows around your face to create a false appearance, blending deception with twilight magic. On a success, your facial features become obscured and shift subtly, making you difficult to identify. You gain +1 boon on rolls to disguise yourself or avoid recognition. Whenever anyone attempts to remember specific details about your appearance, they must first succeed on a Spirit + Perception roll vs. your Resist. On a failure, they can only recall vague, conflicting details.
This spell lasts for a medium duration or until you choose to end it.

name: Shadow Veil
effect:
You create an area of magical shadow, making it hard to see through it. Target any area of dim light in range.
On a success, the magical shadow appears in a close radius around the target area. The area is now considered as absolute darkness.
The shadow lasts a short duration or is broken early if any source of bright light enters the shadow’s area.

name: Whispers of Doubt
effect:
You summon ghostly whispers into the mind of the target, seeding doubt and confusing them.
Weak. The target suffers +0 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. The target suffers +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly confused.
Critical. The target suffers +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is confused for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Cloak of Night
effect:
You clad a creature in a magical cloak made of darkness.
On a success, the creature wearing the cloak gains the following effects:
- You have resistance against frost damage.
- You gain +1 boon on Agility + Stealth rolls to hide or move silently.
The cloak lasts for a medium duration or until discarded by the creature.

name: Curse of Twilight
effect:
You curse a creature with the magical power of twilight, bringing the fear of night into their heart.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- They see in bright light as in dim light.
- They suffer +1 bane on Perception and Initiative rolls.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target suffers a weakness to either frost or psychic damage (choose one) for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target suffers a weakness to both frost and psychic damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Dusk Veil
effect:
You draw the halflight around you like a cloak, and for a heartbeat you are more shadow than flesh. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you gain +2 to your Parry or Dodge against the triggering attack. If you are in dim light or darkness, you can immediately move a close distance without provoking Opportunity Attacks after the attack resolves, melting into the nearest shadow.

name: Haunting Shadows
effect:
You conjure vengeful shadows that follow and torment a target with chilling whispers and ghostly touches. On a success, imaginary spectral shadows begin haunting the target. The target takes +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) at the start of each of their turns. The shadows whisper disturbing thoughts, causing the target to suffer +1 bane on Spirit and Mind rolls.
The haunting lasts for a short duration. The target can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target takes +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) The target takes +6 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Lunar Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of the night. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon emits dim light in close range of it and deals +2 frost damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly slowed. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 frost damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 frost damage instead.

name: Moon Sphere
effect:
You conjure a small orb of freezing coldness resembling a miniature moon.
On a success, the sphere appears at the target location. It also shines dim light in short range of it. Any creature in melee range of it takes +4 frost damage when they first come into contact with it and at the start of each of their turns.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to move the beam a close distance within range. The sphere lasts for a short duration.

name: Shadow Meld
effect:
You submerge into the shadows around you. Cast this spell while standing in dim light or darkness.
On a success, you merge with the shadows and become invisible. While invisible, you are hidden from any creature which hasn‘t an ability to see through invisibility.
This spell lasts for a short duration, until you attack or cast another spell, or until you enter bright light.

name: Aura of Fear
effect: You emanate an aura of primal terror that affects all hostile creatures in close range. On a success, you create an aura that moves with you for a short duration. Any hostile creature that starts their turn within close range of you must roll Spirit + Mysticism vs. your Resist. On a failure, they become frightened of you for the rest of their turn and take +4 psychic damage (ignore AV). A creature can only be affected by this spell once per turn. Creatures that succeed on their test are immune to this spell's fear effect until the spell ends.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- The aura extends in short range around you instead.
- On a failure, each creature takes +8 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Everlasting Night
effect:
You cloud an area in absolute darkness and no light may pierce through it.
On a success, the close area around the target location becomes absolute darkness. Any light source within it immediately goes out.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Moonbeam
effect:
You call down a beam of moonlight at the target location, blinding and freezing anything within it.
On a success, the beam extends in melee range of the target location and a medium distance in height. It also shines dim light in close range of it. Any creatures inside the beam are blinded and take +6 frost damage when they first come into contact with it and at the start of each of their turns.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Quick Action to move the beam a short distance within range. The beam lasts for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The beam deals +8 frost damage instead and shines dim light in short range of it.
(Rank 4) The beam deals +10 frost damage instead and shines dim light in short range of it.
(Rank 5) The beam extends in close range of the target location instead, deals +12 frost damage, and shines dim light in short range of it.

name: Shadow Step
effect:
You fully submerge into the shadows and re-appear at another darkened location. While standing in dim light or darkness, choose any other location you can see in range that is also in dim light or darkness.
On a success, you teleport to the chosen location.

name: Silent Night
effect:
Moonless quiet pools out from your gesture and swallows every sound, as though the night itself held its breath. You create a zone of absolute silence in close range of the target location for up to a medium duration while you concentrate. While it lasts:
- No sound can be made or heard inside the zone, and none crosses its border.
- Creatures inside are deafened and their Stealth is never given away by sound.
- Speech and any effect that must be spoken or heard fails inside the zone.
- No spell can be cast inside the zone, unless cast by silent means such as the Silent Spell metamagic.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The zone's radius extends to short range of the target location instead.
(Rank 4) As Rank 3, and you may anchor the zone to a moving creature or object you touch when casting so it travels with them.
(Rank 5) As Rank 4, and the duration extends to a long duration.

name: Terrors of the Dark
effect:
You fill an area with frightening shadows that take the shape of each creature's worst fears. Choose a close area within range that contains dim light or darkness. Target all hostile creatures within the area.
Weak. Each target is briefly frightened and takes +2 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Each target is frightened for a short duration and takes +4 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Critical. Each target is frightened for a short duration, briefly stunned, and takes +6 psychic damage (ignore AV).
They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened: (Rank 3) Each target takes +4/+8/+12 psychic damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Twilight Bind
effect:
You weave twilight magic into a creature's mind, temporarily turning them from foe to friend through shadow manipulation clouding their perception of reality. The target must be of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism. On a success, the target becomes charmed by you and treats you and your allies as friends for a short duration.
The creature can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early. If the creature takes damage from you or your allies, they can immediately make this roll with +1 boon.
You can only cast this spell once against any individual creature once per day.
heightened: (Rank 3) The spell lasts for a medium duration instead.

name: Nightmare Realm
effect:
You trap targets in a twilight realm of their own fears and doubts, where they manifest monsters of their own mind. Choose a close area within range. Target all hostile creatures within the area.
On a success, the realm persists for a short duration. Use your roll’s result vs. their Resist.
Weak. Each target takes +4 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. Each target takes +8 psychic damage (ignore AV) and is briefly staggered.
Critical. Each target takes +12 psychic damage (ignore AV) and is briefly stunned.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to repeat the roll vs. Resist against all hostile creatures within the nightmare realm.

name: Shadow Clone
effect:
You shape shadows into a perfect duplicate of yourself that can act independently in darkened areas. On a success, you create a shadow clone in any area of dim light or darkness within range. The clone has your appearance but is made of living shadow. It has 1 HP, your Movement, and can perform simple tasks and basic attacks (no Combat Arts or other abilities) using your statistics. The clone can only exist in dim light or darkness. If it enters bright light, it is immediately destroyed.
As a Quick Action, you can switch places with your shadow clone, instantly teleporting to its location while it appears where you were standing.
The clone lasts for a medium duration, until destroyed, or until you dismiss it. You can command the clone telepathically.

name: Embrace of Night
effect:
You merge temporarily with the essence of twilight, gaining supernatural abilities in the balance between light and dark. On a success, you gain the following effects:
- You become invisible while in dim light or darkness
- You gain resistance to psychic and frost damage
- You can pass through solid objects as if they were difficult terrain while invisible
- Your twilight mysticism spells cost 2 less Focus (minimum 0)
This transformation lasts for a short duration.

name: Life Shield
effect:
On a success, the target is surrounded by a near invisible shield of life force. The shield grants 5 temporary HP and lasts until reduced to 0, or expires after a short duration.
Any single creature can only benefit from this spell once per scene.

name: Restore Life
effect:
You let vitalizing energy flow into your target, restoring a living creatures health or harming an undead creature.
When targeting a living creature, roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). When targeting an undead creature, roll vs. their Resist.
Weak. Restore +2 HP to your target or deal +3 radiant damage against an undead target.
Strong. Restore +4 HP to your target or deal +6 radiant damage against an undead target.
Critical. Restore +6 HP to your target or deal +9 radiant damage against an undead target.

name: Verdant Blast
effect:
You form a condensed mass of positive energy, blasting the enemy and healing nearby allies.
Weak. Deal +2 radiant damage.
Strong. Deal +4 radiant damage and restore 2 HP to allies in melee range of the target.
Critical. Deal +6 radiant damage and restore 4 HP to allies in melee range of the target.

name: Blessing of Life
effect:
You bless a creature with the magical power of life, bolstering their vitality.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You gain 5 temporary HP. They replenish after a short break.
- You gain +1 boon on Fortitude rolls.
This spell lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) You gain 10 temporary HP instead.
(Rank 3) You gain 20 temporary HP instead.

name: Healing Touch
effect:
You focus life-giving magic into an injury, mending it quickly or taking longer to close more grievous injuries.
You can cast this spell using an Action or casting it as a ritual (minutes).
Action. Restore +4/8/12 HP to your target.
Ritual (minutes). On a success, you heal one Wound from a creature. A creature can only benefit from one Wound-healing spell effect per day.
heightened:
(Rank 2) When using your Action, restore +6/12/18 HP instead. When casting this spell as a ritual (minutes) instead, heal up to two Wounds from a creature.
(Rank 3) When using your Action, restore +8/16/24 HP instead. When casting this spell as a ritual (minutes) instead, heal up to three Wounds from a creature.

name: Overflow of Life
effect:
You overwork the vital system of the target, returning them to consciousness or causing them a heart attack if already conscious. The target must be a living creature.
Weak. If the target is dying, they stop dying but remain unconscious. If the target is unconscious, they wake up. If the target is conscious, they take +3 radiant damage.
Strong. If the target is dying, they stop dying and wake up. If the target is unconscious, they wake up. If the target is conscious, they take +6 radiant damage.
Critical. If the target is dying, they stop dying, wake up, and one of their Injuries is instantly treated (their choice). If the target is unconscious, they wake up. If the target is conscious, they take +9 radiant damage.
heightened:
(Rank 2) If the target is conscious, they take +5/10/15 radiant damage instead.
(Rank 3) If the target is conscious, they take +7/14/21 radiant damage instead.

name: Rapid Vitality
effect:
You quickly envigorate a creature. Cast this spell on your turn.
Weak. Restore +2 HP to your target.
Strong. Restore +4 HP to your target.
Critical. Restore +6 HP to your target.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Restore +3/6/9 HP instead.
(Rank 3) Restore +4/8/12 HP instead.
(Rank 4) Restore +5/10/15 HP instead.
(Rank 5) Restore +6/12/18 HP instead.

name: Vitalizing Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of vitality. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, any strong or critical hit with the weapon restores 4 HP to the wielder. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) On a strong or critical hit, restore 6 HP instead.
(Rank 3) On a strong or critical hit, restore 8 HP instead.

name: Aid
effect:
Bolster your allies’ toughness with a blessing.
On a success, choose up to four creatures in range. They each gain 10 temporary HP for a long duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) Each target gains 20 temporary HP instead.

name: Cleanse
effect: Relieve a creature from any negative conditions. Cast this spell on your turn. Target a willing creature. On a success, remove the following conditions from the target: bleeding, burning, blinded, charmed, confused, dazed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, silenced, slowed, staggered, and stunned. This doesn’t remove conditions applied by a continuous effect that affects the target indirectly, such as an persistent area effect.
heightened: (Rank 3) Choose an additional willing target in range.

name: Detect Life
effect:
You close your eyes and open your senses to the warm current of life itself, feeling every living spark around you like embers in the dark.
On a success, for up to a short duration while you concentrate, you sense the presence, direction, and Size category of every living creature within medium range of you. You learn each creature's exact Size (tiny up to colossal) but not what kind of creature they are. This sense does not depend on sight and reaches through walls, cover, and darkness. Undead, constructs, and other unliving things are not revealed by this spell. A creature standing among many others, such as in a dense crowd or a herd, blends into a single mass of life unless within close range of you.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The spell reaches within long range of you instead, and you also sense each creature's vitality: whether they are unharmed, wounded, or close to death.
(Rank 4) The spell reaches within long range of you, you sense each creature's vitality, and you also recognize each sensed creature's type (such as Beast, Humanoid, or Plant).

name: Hallow Ground
effect:
You hallow the ground around you, healing all allies standing on it.
On a success, each ally in range of the hallow ground restores +2 HP when you cast the spell and at the start of each of their turns.
The hallow ground remains stationary at the position where you cast it for a short duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) Restore +4 HP instead.

name: Hallowed Rest
effect:
You consecrate your camp with the soft glow of communal life energy, blessing all who sleep within its warmth.
On a success, you bless the area in short range of the target location for a long duration. Choose any number of creatures within the blessed area — they are warded by the blessing. Warded creatures completing a night’s rest within the blessed area gain the following benefits:
- They remove 1 additional point of Fatigue.
- They gain +1 boon on their roll to heal a treated Wound.
- If their rest is reduced to a bad night, they still regain all of their Focus.

name: Healing Burst
effect:
You conjure a wave of healing energy, restoring the endurance of all allies within it.
Weak. Restore +3 HP to each ally.
Strong. Restore +6 HP to each ally.
Critical. Restore +9 HP to each ally.
heightened: (Rank 3) Restore +4/8/12 HP instead.

name: Abundance of Life
effect:
You create a powerful zone of life energy that continuously restores vitality to your allies.
On a success, you create the zone in short range of the target location. While the spell is active, apply the following effects:
- Allies within the zone restores +4 HP when you cast the spell and at the start of each of their turns.
- When an ally within the zone is affected by any healing effect other than from this spell, increase that healing by 4 HP.
- Any ally within the zone suffering one or more Wounds can use their Action to bathe in the zone’s life force and instantly heal one of their Wounds and regain +8 HP. A creature can only benefit from one Wound-healing spell effect per day.
The zone lasts for a short duration.

name: Vitality Surge
effect:
You unleash a devastating explosion of pure life force that overwhelms your enemies' vital systems while gently reinvigorating your allies.
Target all hostile creatures and your allies within the spell’s area. Compare the roll against a very hard Difficulty (12) to calculate the SL for your allies.
Weak. Deal +4 radiant damage to each hostile target. Restore +3 HP to each ally.
Strong. Deal +8 radiant damage to each hostile target and they are briefly dazed. Restore +6 HP to each ally.
Critical. Deal +12 radiant damage to each hostile target and they are briefly stunned. Restore +9 HP to each ally.

name: Chill Touch
effect:
Your touch carries the cold of the grave, sapping warmth and vitality from your target.
Weak. Deal +3 frost damage.
Strong. Deal +6 frost damage and the target briefly cannot regain HP.
Critical. Deal +9 frost damage and the target briefly cannot regain HP.

name: Decay
effect:
You accelerate the natural decay of organic matter, causing wood to rot, food to spoil, and cloth to fray. You can’t target objects carried, worn, or wielded by a creature. On a success, you can rapidly age and decay a small area of non-living organic materials (such as wooden objects, food, or clothing) within range. This spell affects objects up to medium Size and can be used to:
- Spoil food or drink, making it inedible (remove 1 use from rations)
- Weaken wooden doors, weapons, or structures (make a Durability check for that object)
- Age clothing or leather to the point of tearing

name: Enfeebling Touch
effect:
You summon a swirl of negative energy, engulfing the target and draining their strength.
Weak. Deal +2 necrotic damage.
Strong. Deal +4 necrotic damage and briefly reduce their Strength die by one step.
Critical. Deal +6 necrotic damage and reduce their Strength die by one step for a short duration. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Glimpse of Mortality
effect:
You project a scene of the target‘s final moments before death into their mind, in which they die in agony at the hands of yourself and your allies.
Weak. The target suffers +0 psychic damage (ignore AV).
Strong. The target suffers +2 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is briefly frightened.
Critical. The target suffers +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and the target is frightened for a short time. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Spared from Death
effect:
You help a dying creature in range to cling onto life.
Weak. +1 to the dice result of the dying creature.
Strong. +2 to the dice result of the dying creature.
Critical. You stop the creature from dying, but they remain unconscious.

name: Blood Shards
effect:
You conjure razor-sharp crystallized blood shards and hurl them at your target, piercing their flesh and drawing life force back to you.
Weak. Deal +3 necrotic damage and regain 2 HP.
Strong. Deal +6 necrotic damage and regain 4 HP.
Critical. Deal +9 necrotic damage and regain 6 HP.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +5/+10/+15 necrotic damage and regain 3/6/9 HP instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +7/+14/+21 necrotic damage and regain 4/8/12 HP instead.

name: Curse of Death
effect:
You curse a creature with the magical power of death, withering their mortal body.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- Their max. HP are reduced by 5.
- They can't regain HP.
- They suffer +1 bane on Fortitude rolls.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target suffers a weakness to either necrotic or poison damage (choose one) for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target suffers a weakness to both necrotic and poison damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Death's Rebuke
effect:
You meet the blow with the grave's patience, and what harms you withers in answer. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you gain +2 to your Parry or Dodge against the triggering attack. Decay crawls back along the strike and the attacker takes 2 necrotic damage (ignore AV), whether their attack hits you or not.

name: Early Grave
effect:
Skeletal hands sprout from the ground, scratching at the target and attempting to pull them below the earth.
Weak. Deal +3 necrotic damage.
Strong. Deal +6 necrotic damage and the target is grappled by the hands.
Critical. Deal +9 necrotic damage and the target is grappled and briefly restrained by the hands.

name: Necrotic Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of death. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 necrotic damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack’s target also suffers 2 lasting necrotic damage for a short duration. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 necrotic damage instead. On a strong or critical hit, inflict 4 lasting necrotic damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 necrotic damage instead. On a strong or critical hit, inflict 6 lasting necrotic damage instead.

name: Rotting Grasp
effect:
Your touch carries the power of decay, causing flesh to wither and rot where you grasp your target.
Weak. Deal +4 necrotic damage and the target is briefly poisoned.
Strong. Deal +8 necrotic damage and the target is briefly poisoned and staggered.
Critical. Deal +12 necrotic damage and the target is poisoned for a short duration. While poisoned by this effect, they are also staggered. They can roll Strength + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 necrotic damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 necrotic damage instead.

name: Shivering Ray
effect:
You shoot a icy blue beam from the tip of your finger, rigidifying the target.
Weak. You deal +3 frost damage.
Strong. You deal +6 frost damage and the target is briefly slowed.
Critical. You deal +9 frost damage and the target is briefly dazed and slowed.

name: Circle of Death
effect:
You draw a mystical circle filled with symbols of mortality, creating a zone where death is inevitable. On a success, you create the circle in close range of the target location. While the circle remains, any creature that starts their turn within it takes +4 necrotic damage (ignore AV) and can’t regain HP. Any undead creature takes +4 radiant damage (ignore AV) instead, as their souls long to return to the grave.
The circle lasts for a short duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) Each creature takes +6 necrotic or radiant damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Cloud of Sickness
effect:
You summon a cloud of necrotic energy, sickening creatures within it. The cloud extends in a close area around the target location and targets all living creatures within it.
Weak. Deal +3 necrotic damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 necrotic damage to each target.
Critical. Deal +9 necrotic damage to each target.
Any creature is also poisoned while inside the cloud’s area.
The cloud lasts for a short duration. It can also be expelled early by any effect causing strong winds in the same area of influence.
heightened: (Rank 3) Deal +4/+8/+12 necrotic damage to each target instead.

name: Death's Door
effect:
You show the target a vision of their own death, paralyzing them with the certainty of mortality. The target must be of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism.
Weak. The target takes +4 psychic damage (ignore AV) and is briefly stunned.
Strong. The target takes +8 psychic damage (ignore AV) and is stunned for a short duration.
Critical. The target takes +12 psychic damage (ignore AV) and is paralyzed for a short duration. They can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.
If they take damage from any source, they can immediately make the roll with +1 boon.
You can only cast this spell once against any creature per scene.

name: Grave’s Bloom
effect:
You channel the essence of natural toxins into an area, creating a blooming field of highly poisonous flowers, which then erupt into a cloud of death. Choose a close area within range. Target all living creatures within the area.
Weak. Each target takes +4 poison damage and is briefly poisoned.
Strong. Each target takes +8 poison damage and is poisoned for a short duration.
Critical. Each target takes +12 poison damage, is briefly confused, and is poisoned for a short duration.
They can roll Strength + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Acid Splash
effect:
You conjure a short spray of acid, burning through the target’s surface.
Weak. Deal +2 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +4 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Critical. Deal +6 acid damage (ignore 1/2 AV).

name: Bestial Adaptation
effect:
You adopt the physical characteristics of one chosen type of animal. Cast this spell as a Quick Action on your turn. Choose one of the following:
- Graceful (You can move through difficult terrain without penalty)
- Perceptive (+1 boon on Perception rolls)
- Strong (+1 boon on Strength + Athletics rolls, +2 damage on unarmed attacks)
- Tough (+2 AV (armor bonus))
On a success, you gain the chosen effect. If you cast this spell multiple times, you only retain the newest option chosen. This spell lasts for a short duration.

name: Lashing Vine
effect:
You animate a natural vine or let one spring from the ground in range. The vine can either lash at (vs. Parry) or grab (vs. Parry or Dodge) any target close to it. The vine counts as a medium creature for grappling.
Weak. Deal +2 damage (lash) or grapple the target (grab).
Strong. Deal +4 damage (lash) or deal +0 damage and grapple the target (grab).
Critical. Deal +6 damage (lash) or deal +2 damage, grapple, and briefly restrain the target (grab).
if you continue to cast this spell over multiple turns, the vine can remain for the duration. Otherwise the vine quickly withers and vanishes after using this spell.
You can also use the vine to grab, pull, or throw smaller objects.

name: Poison Mist
effect:
Spray a cloud of poisonous mist against your target.
Weak. Deal +2 poison damage.
Strong. Deal +4 poison damage and the target is briefly poisoned.
Critical. Deal +6 poison damage and the target is poisoned for a short duration. They can roll Strength + Fortitude vs. your Resist at the end of their turns to end the effect early.

name: Rejuvenation
effect:
Conjure magical energy into one creature in range, rapidly increasing their natural regenerative capacity.
Weak. The target shortly regains +0 HP at the start of each of their turns.
Strong. The target shortly regains +2 HP at the start of each of their turns.
Critical. The target shortly regains +4 HP at the start of each of their turns.

name: Shillelagh
effect: You imbue a wooden weapon with nature’s power to strike your enemy with. Choose any crush weapon you are holding that is made from wood. Roll an attack with that weapon using Spirit + Mysticism. On a hit, use your spell power instead of the attack's base damage.

name: Bark Shield
effect:
Rough bark sheathes your skin in an instant, hard as old oak. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you gain +2 to your Parry or Dodge against the triggering attack. The bark clings to you afterwards for a moment, granting you +1 AV briefly.

name: Beast Form
effect:
You shape shift into the form of a land-based animal. You can change into any animal form you encountered before. For this, reference the Tier 1 or lower Companion Statistics and pick any valid Animal Trait from the Animal Companions section.
Apply the given statistics in the following way:
- Pick any Size valid for the beast form’s Tier.
- You gain half of the HP your beast form would have as an Animal Companion as temporary HP (you keep your own current and max. HP).
- Apply the beast form’s AV bonus as an armor bonus.
- Replace your Strength and Agility with that of your beast form if they are higher. Your Spirit and Mind aren’t changed.
- You keep your own skill ranks and don’t use those of the beast form.
- Apply the beast form’s weapon damage to any attacks made with natural weapons. If you have any bonuses to the damage of these attacks, you can apply them normally.
- You gain the amount of base Movement per turn of your beast form, unless your normal Movement is higher.
- You can use any abilities your beast form’s animal traits provide.
You can’t cast spells while in beast form, except for re-casting this spell to extend it‘s duration without interruption. You can still speak and understand any languages you would normally while in your beast form. Your beast form lasts for a medium duration, until you end it willingly, or until you fall unconscious.
heightened:
(Rank 2) You can choose from the Companion Statistics up to Tier 2.
(Rank 3) You can choose from the Companion Statistics up to Tier 3.

name: Blessing of Nature
effect:
You bless a creature with the magical power of nature, whereby plants and animals act benevolently towards them.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You can move through difficult terrain without penalty.
- You gain +1 boon on Athletics and Survival rolls.
This spell lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target gains resistance to acid and poison damage for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target gains resistance to acid, frost, lightning, and poison damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Mesh of Vines
effect:
Instantly sprout a mesh of spiked vines, covering the target location. On a success, spiked vines fill an area close to the target location. Target each creature in the area and use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
Weak. Deal +0 damage against each target.
Strong. Deal +2 damage against each target and they are grappled by the vines.
Critical. Deal +2 damage against each target and they are grappled and restrained by the vines.
The area is also considered difficult terrain. The vines last for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The vines deal +2/+4/+6 damage against each target instead.
(Rank 3) The vines deal +4/+8/+12 damage against each target instead.

name: Purify Water
effect:
You cleanse and purify water, removing contaminants through natural magic. Choose a body of water within close range of the target location. This spell has no effect on magical liquids or creatures made of liquid.
On a success, you remove all poisons, diseases, and other harmful contaminants from the water, making it safe to drink. Any creatures who drink from the water during a short duration after you cast this spell also gain 5 temporary HP.

name: Rock Throw
effect:
Magically animate a large stone and throw it against your target in range.
Weak. Deal +3 damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +6 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and the target is pushed close.
Critical. Deal +9 damage (ignore 1/2 AV), the target is pushed close and falls prone.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +5/+10/+15 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +7/+14/+21 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Sticks to Snakes
effect:
You throw out a bundle of sticks, which then turn into withering snakes under your control. You must roll a Supply check for materials (Quality 2 or higher) when casting this spell.
On a success, you summon a swarm of snakes at the target location under your control (treat them as a companion for combat).
The swarm remains for a medium duration, until dismissed by you, or when killed.

name: Venomous Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of a serpent‘s fangs. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 poison damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly poisoned. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 poison damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 poison damage instead.

name: Wild Companion
effect:
You can’t cast this spell if you haven’t learned the “Animal Companion” talent.
You set up a ritual to summon a spiritual beast of the land. As part of casting this spell, you must also spend 100 coins in incense and other sacred ingredients (they are spend even on a failure). Follow the normal rules, described in the “Animal Companion” talent for choosing a form for your animal companion (you can still only control as many animal companions, as you are allowed by the talent).
On a success, you summon a spiritual being in your chosen animal form. Apply the following traits to it:
- It’s creature type becomes “spirit (primal)”
- +1d Spirit, +1d Mind
- +1 Resist
You also have a psychic connection to your companion, as long as the two of you are on the same sphere of existence. This connection manifests itself as an intuitive sharing of emotions. You can use this connection to tell your companion what to do and where to go nonverbally. You can also spend your turn meditating to fully experience all senses of your companion. While meditating this way, you are considered unconscious and are unaware of your own body‘s surroundings.
Any creature capable of sensing magical auras will notice the primal nature of your companion. The companion remains until killed, but can be re-summoned with another ritual. You can choose the same or a new form for your companion each time you cast this spell.
heightened:
(Rank 2) As part of casting this spell, you must spend 200 coins instead. Also apply the following traits to your companion:
- It can cast any rank 0 or 1 spells you know. It casts them with it’s Spirit + your Nature. Instead of spending Focus, it can only cast a number of rank 1 spells per day equal to your Nature.
(Rank 3) As part of casting this spell, you must spend 500 coins instead. Also apply the following traits to your companion:
- It can cast any rank 0, 1, or 2 spells you know (rank 1 spells are heightened to rank 2). It casts them with it’s Spirit + your Nature. Instead of spending Focus, it can only cast a combined number of rank 1 or 2 spells per day equal to your Nature.

name: Heat Metal
effect:
You let a metallic object rapidly increase in temperature, harming anyone touching it.
You must be not wearing any armor or weapon primarily made from a metal to cast this spell. Choose any metallic object or creature of up to medium Size.
On a success, the target becomes burning hot. Any creature holding or wearing the object suffers +2 fire damage (ignore AV) at the start of each turn they continue to do so.
If the target is a weapon, it imposes +1 bane on attacks with it.
If the target is a piece of armor or a helmet, it‘s AV bonus is halved.
This spell lasts for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- You can also target objects with up to large Size.
- The object inflicts +4 fire damage (ignore AV) each turn instead.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- You can also target objects with up to huge Size.
- The object inflicts +6 fire damage (ignore AV) each turn instead.

name: Law of the Strongest
effect:
You fill a creature‘s body with empowering nature magic. The target has to be a creature of medium or smaller Size.
On a success, the target‘s body grows one Size larger along with all of their worn equipment and held items (as long as they stay in contact with their body). While enlarged by this spell, they gain the following effects:
- Gain 5 temporary HP.
- Gain +1 boon on Strength rolls.
- You gain +1 Parry and lose -1 Dodge.
- You gain +1 AV.
- You deal +2 damage with Strength-based attacks.
This spell lasts for a short duration or until the target falls unconscious.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The target gains the following effects instead:
- Gain 10 temporary HP.
- Gain +1 boon on Strength rolls.
- You gain +2 Parry and lose -2 Dodge.
- You gain +2 AV.
- You deal +4 damage with Strength-based attacks.

name: Living Plants
effect:
You manipulate nearby plants to grow rapidly and attack your enemies or form structures. Choose any location in range. On a success, plants grow and animate under your control, filling an area close to the target location.
You can use your Action during each of your turns to command the plants to perform one of the following abilities:
Attack. Target any number of creatures in the area and use your roll's result vs. their Dodge. Deal +2/+4/+6 damage against each target.
Restrain. Target any number of creatures in the area and use your roll's result vs. their Dodge. On a success, each target is grappled. On a strong or critical success, each target is grappled and restrained.
If you choose any other ability during one of your turns, this ability ends.
Shape. Form the plants into a simple structure (such as a shelter, bridge, or barrier). The structure is solid and provides cover. If you end the spell while the plants are shaped, the shape remains stable for a long duration, after which the plants wither and decay.
The plants last for a medium duration or until you dismiss them.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- The plants fill in short range of the target location.
- The plants deal +4/+8/+12 damage with attacks instead.

name: Melt Ground
effect:
You cause the ground in the target area to become soft and muddy, creating a natural trap. On a success, the ground in an area close to the target location becomes muddy and creatures sink slightly into it.
The muddy area is difficult terrain. Also, when any creature attempts to move though the muddy area for the first time during their turn, they have to first succeed on a Strength + Athletics test vs. this spell’s target Difficulty. If they fail, their Movement becomes 0 for the rest of the turn.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) The affected area extends to medium range from the target location instead.

name: Nature’s Passage
effect:
You conjure up an aura around you, gently warping plants and vegetation in your vicinity as you move.
On a success, apply the following effects:
- You can Dash as a Quick Action on your turns.
- Any difficult terrain in short distance of you temporarily becomes normal terrain.
- You gain +1 boon on Navigation rolls during travel.
This spell lasts for a long duration.

name: Stone Skin
effect:
You create a protective shell of hardened rock plates around a creature within range. On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- The rock plates absorbs any non-psychic damage inflicted on you. When the rock plates have absorbed a total of 20 HP, they break and the spell ends.
- Your unarmed attacks deal +2 damage.
- You gain +1 Parry and lose -1 Dodge.
- You can’t use more than 1 Movement per turn.
heightened:
(Rank 3) The target gains the following effects instead:
- The rock plates absorbs any non-psychic damage inflicted on you. When the rock plates have absorbed a total of 30 HP, they break and the spell ends.
- Your unarmed attacks deal +4 damage.
- You gain +1 Parry and lose -1 Dodge.
- You can’t use more than 1 Movement per turn.
(Rank 4) The target gains the following effects instead:
- The rock plates absorbs any non-psychic damage inflicted on you. When the rock plates have absorbed a total of 40 HP, they break and the spell ends.
- Your unarmed attacks deal +6 damage.
- You gain +1 Parry and lose -1 Dodge.
- You can’t use more than 1 Movement per turn.
(Rank 5) The target gains the following effects instead:
- The rock plates absorbs any non-psychic damage inflicted on you. When the rock plates have absorbed a total of 50 HP, they break and the spell ends.
- Your unarmed attacks deal +8 damage.
- You gain +1 Parry and lose -1 Dodge.
- You can’t use more than 1 Movement per turn.

name: Speak with Animals and Plants
effect:
You attune yourself to the natural consciousness of nearby flora and fauna, allowing communication.
On a success, you can understand and speak with animals and plants within range. Plants have limited perception and knowledge, typically aware only of events in their immediate vicinity involving weather, other plants, and obvious physical interactions. Animals are more perceptive but have limited intelligence and memory.
You can ask each individual plant or friendly animal three questions, which they will answer to the best of their ability. Answers are filtered through the creature's perspective and intelligence. A plant might recall a person passing by but not their face. A bird might remember colorful clothing but not what was said. Any non-friendly animal will not be beholden to answer any of your questions.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.

name: Sudden Growth
effect:
You dramatically accelerate the growth of plant life in the target area.
On a success, choose one of the following effects:
Barrier. Create a wall of tough plants which covers a close distance in width and height. The barrier has 20 HP, 2 AV (light), 8 Defense, and provides cover. Climbing the barrier requires a Strength + Athletics roll.
Platform. Create a stable platform of intertwined branches covering up to a close area that can support creature with medium Size or smaller. The platform can grow up to a short distance above ground level. Any creatures standing on the space where the branches grow into the air are safely lifted up with it. For damaging the platform, use the same statistics as for the Barrier option.
Harvest. Magically fertilize soil within medium range of the target area. Any fruit, vegetable, or other crop on that soil will be immune against disease and grow twice as fast.
When you choose Barrier or Platform, the plants remain for a short duration. If you choose Harvest, the effect remains until the plants are fully grown.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Apply these effects for each option:
Barrier. The wall can cover up to a short distance in width and height instead. It also has 30 HP and 4 AV instead.
Platform. The platform can grow up to a medium distance above ground level instead.
Harvest. Fertilize soil within long range of the target area instead.

name: Thorn Barrage
effect:
You launch a volley of magically-created thorns that tear through multiple targets in a line.
Target all creatures in a straight line from you up to a target point within range.
Weak. Deal +3 damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 damage to each target and they are bleeding (2).
Critical. Deal +9 damage to each target and they are bleeding (4).
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 damage to each target and inflict bleeding (-/4/6) instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Toxic Mist
effect:
You create a cone of toxic fog that spreads from your position. Target all creatures within the spell’s area.
Weak. Deal +2 poison damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target.
Strong. Deal +4 poison damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are poisoned for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +6 poison damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target and they are poisoned for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +3/+6/+9 poison damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Increase the spell’s range to medium.

name: Water Prison
effect:
You conjure a sphere of water that engulfs and traps a target of up to medium Size, causing them to begin drowning.
On a success, the target is grappled and restrained by the sphere of water and suffocating until they escape.
This spell lasts for a short duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) You can target a creature of up to large Size instead.

name: Animal Messenger
effect:
You enchant a small, friendly animal to carry a message for you.
On a success, you can send a small animal (such as a bird, squirrel, or rat) to a specific recipient or location you know within one day’s distance. You must have personally seen the recipient or location at least once.
You can attach a small physical object with low weight to the animal or have it memorize a spoken message of up to 25 words. The animal will travel at its normal speed but with supernatural determination and direction sense. It will attempt to deliver the message or item directly to the intended recipient, waiting up to one day for a good opportunity.

name: Fungal Growth
effect:
You cause a rapid eruption of psychoactive fungi in the target area.
On a success, magical mushrooms sprout throughout an area close to the target location. The area becomes difficult terrain and is filled with hallucinogenic spores. Any creature that starts its turn in the area or enters it for the first time on its turn must succeed on a Spirit + Fortitude roll vs. your Resist or become briefly confused.
The fungi remain for up to a medium duration and even if you move out of range of the area.

name: Impalement
effect:
You cause a massive stone spike to erupt from the ground beneath your target.
Weak. Deal +6 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to the target.
Strong. Deal +12 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to the target and they are pushed up to a short distance upward or away from you.
Critical. Deal +18 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to the target and they are pushed up to a short distance upward or away from you.

name: Insect Swarm
effect:
You summon a dense cloud of biting and stinging insects that fill an area within short range from the target location.
On a success, the insect swarm appears and attacks all creatures within the area that you choose. Use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
On a success, each target takes +4 damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and is briefly distracted by the insects.
On each of your following turns, you can use your Action to repeat the roll vs. Dodge against any amount of creatures within the swarm’s area to repeat the spell’s effect. Before or after the roll, you can choose to move the swarm a short distance within range.
The swarm is immune against attacks but if they would suffer any amount of fire damage, roll a Concentration test for this spell as if you took that damage.
The swarm remains for a short duration.

name: Life from Stone
effect:
You infuse a large or smaller object or structure made from stone or wood with primal energy, bringing it to life as a construct.
On a success, the target object animates and becomes a creature under your control with statistics equal to a Tier 3 companion with the Size of the original object. In addition, use the following traits for it:
HP. +10 HP
AV. + Tier (natural heavy)
Attributes. +1d Strength, -1d Agility
Defenses. +1 Parry, -1 Dodge
Skills. Fighting (-1 rank), Fortitude
Immunities. poison damage, poisoned, bleeding, prone, unconscious
Resistances. psychic
Weaknesses. blast
Attacks
- Slam (crush). On a strong or critical hit, the target falls prone.
The construct remains animated for a short duration, until dismissed by you, or until it’s HP are reduced to 0.

name: Nature's Judgment
effect:
You mark a creature with a primal stigma, causing nature itself to turn against them.
On a success, the target is marked by your primal magic. While marked, the target suffers the following effects:
- Plants within medium range of the target actively hamper its movement, making all natural terrain difficult terrain for them.
- The target suffers +1 bane on all Strength and Agility rolls as branches snag, roots trip, and stones shift underfoot.
- Any animal not friendly towards the creature is automatically hostile towards them. If an animal is friendly towards them, they have to roll Spirit + Fortitude against your Resist. If they fail, they also turn hostile against the marked target. All hostile animals gain +1 boon on attacks against them.
- The target cannot benefit from cover provided by natural materials (such as trees, bushes, or stones).
The mark lasts for up to a medium duration.

name: Petrification
effect:
You turn a creature into inanimate stone. The target has to be of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism. If the creature is at full HP, you suffer +1 bane on the roll.
On a success, the target starts to turn to stone and is stunned.
At the end of their next turn, repeat the roll for this spell. If you succeed, the petrification continues and they are now paralyzed.
At the end of their turn after that, repeat the roll for this spell again. If you succeed again, they are fully petrified.
A petrified creature is paralyzed and unconscious. A petrified creature also has 10 AV (heavy natural armor) unless they had more AV normally.
The creature remains petrified while you remain concentrated on the spell. When the creature takes damage during this time, make a Concentration roll as if you took the same amount of damage. After a medium duration concentrating on this spell, the petrification becomes permanent and has to be lifted by an effect that can break curses or petrification (for example, the Break Curse spell or a Potion of Stone to Flesh).

name: Polymorph
effect:
You unravel a creature's shape and re-weave it into the form of a harmless beast. Target one creature of a tier equal to or lower than your Mysticism. Against a willing creature, roll vs. moderate difficulty (8). Against an unwilling creature, roll vs. their Resist.
On a success, the target's body transforms into a tiny or small harmless beast of your choice, such as a mouse, toad, or sheep, along with all their worn and held equipment. You must choose a form that can naturally survive at the target's current location. Underwater you can only choose aquatic forms. While transformed, they use the statistics of a Tier 0 companion of the chosen form, they keep their own HP, and they cannot speak or cast spells. They still act on their turns and can move, flee, hide, or attack as the beast allows.
At the end of each of their turns, an unwilling target can roll Spirit + Fortitude vs. your Resist to end the effect early. Any physical disturbance grants them an immediate additional roll, such as being grabbed, carried, shoved, thrown, or taking any damage. A willing target can instead end the effect on themselves at will. When the spell ends, the target returns to their true form in the same space, with the same HP they currently have. This spell lasts for a short duration.

name: Stone Pillar
effect:
You press your palm to the ground and call the bones of the earth upward, and a column of living rock rises with a deep grinding groan.
On a success, a pillar of solid stone rises from an unoccupied surface of earth, stone, or clay at the target location. The pillar is up to close range wide and rises up to a short distance high, with a flat top that creatures can stand on. You choose its exact height when it rises. Any creature standing on the pillar is carried up with it. An unwilling creature can roll Agility + Athletics vs. your Resist to leap off before it rises, landing prone in an adjacent space.
If the pillar meets a ceiling or other solid obstacle, it stops. A creature caught between the pillar and the obstacle takes 8 damage (ignore AV) and is squeezed into the gap, which counts as a constricted area.
The pillar is ordinary stone. It remains after the spell, can be climbed with a Strength + Athletics roll, and can only be removed by mundane means or magic that affects stone. When you cast this spell again, all pillars from your previous casting crumble into harmless rubble as the new one rises.
heightened:
(Rank 4) The pillar can rise up to a medium distance high instead, and you may raise up to two pillars with one casting, each up to close range wide and placed anywhere in range. A creature caught between a pillar and an obstacle takes 10 damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 5) You may raise up to three pillars with one casting, each up to close range wide, each rising up to a medium distance high, and placed anywhere in range. A creature caught between a pillar and an obstacle takes 12 damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Control Weather
effect:
You commune with sky and season, and the weather bends by slow degrees to the shape of your will. Through a ritual of minutes you begin to turn the prevailing weather across the surrounding region, then hold and deepen it for as long as you concentrate (up to a day of dedicated tending). You may shift the weather one stage at a time toward a chosen condition, never conjuring what the season and land could not plausibly produce. Choose the target condition:
- Clear. Skies open and the air stills. Any weather-based obscurement, difficult terrain, or wind effect across the region ends, and rolls to travel or to see over long distances through the region gain +1 boon.
- Rain. A steady downpour soaks the region. The land beyond short range is Lightly Obscured, unsheltered open flames are extinguished and mundane fires struggle to catch, and empty vessels and cisterns fill with clean water.
- Storm. A tempest of driving rain and wind builds. The region is difficult terrain (including for flight) and ranged attacks through it suffer +1 bane. A creature that travels through the storm without shelter gains +1 Fatigue for the day.
- Fog. A thick mist settles. The land beyond short range is Heavily Obscured, and creatures gain +1 boon on Stealth rolls to hide within it.
- Heat or Cold. The air turns oppressively hot or bitterly cold. A creature that goes a day without proper protection (shade and water against the heat, warmth against the cold) gains +1 Fatigue.
Ending your concentration lets the natural weather reassert itself over the following hours.

name: Tree Stride
effect:
You step into the living wood and the tree takes you in like an old friend, passing you along the green road to emerge far away. You enter a living tree at least your own size that you touch. While the spell lasts, up to a medium duration while you concentrate, you may step into such a tree and emerge from another living tree of similar size within extreme range as your movement, once per turn.
When you enter a tree, your awareness spreads through the green road and you sense every living tree of similar size within range, even those out of sight. To travel, name where you wish to emerge, such as a tree you can see, a known landmark, or a direction and rough distance, and you step out of the nearest suitable tree to that point. If no tree large enough stands within range of your chosen destination, you sense this before you commit and simply do not step, losing nothing. The GM tells you what trees are within reach whenever the surroundings make it uncertain, such as sparse woodland, a lone orchard, or open country where few or none may qualify.
When the spell ends or your concentration breaks, you are expelled from the nearest tree unharmed.
heightened: (Rank 5) You may carry up to five willing creatures who touch you through the green road with each stride, and the range extends across any forested expanse in the same region.

name: Gust
effect:
You summon a vortex of swirling air, clashing against your target and pulling them off their feet.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV).
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target close.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) and push the target a short distance.
You can also cast this spell to create a continuous stream of air from your palm that has enough pressure to move small, loose objects in range.

name: Static Shock
effect:
You generate a small electric discharge from your fingertip against a target.
Weak. Deal +2 lightning damage.
Strong. Deal +4 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +2 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
Critical. Deal +6 lightning damage to the target and you can deal +4 lightning damage to one other creature in melee range of the target.
You can also cast this spell to ignite flammable materials in range, such as tinder or straw.

name: Wind Slash
effect:
Hurl a blade of compressed air against your target.
Weak. Deal +0 damage or +4 damage against a target in light or no armor.
Strong. Deal +2 damage or +6 damage against a target in light or no armor.
Critical. Deal +4 damage or +8 damage against a target in light or no armor.
You can also use this spell to chip off or cut parts of softer materials such as wood or papyrus in range.

name: Bursting Crackle
effect:
You gather an electric charge at a single point within range and release it with a powerful blast, creating loud thunder. Target all creatures in melee range of the target location.
Weak. Deal +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target.
Strong. Deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target and they are briefly dazed and deafened.
Critical. Deal +6 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) against each target and they are briefly dazed and deafened.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Target all creatures in close range of the target location instead.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +6/+12/+18 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to each target instead.
- Target all creatures in short range of the target location instead.

name: Curse of Tempest
effect:
You curse a creature with the magical power of tempest, turning nature itself against them.
On a success, the creature suffers the following effects:
- While moving in natural environments, it is always considered difficult terrain for them.
- They suffer +1 bane on Athletics and Survival rolls.
This curse lasts for a long duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The target suffers a weakness to blast, fire, or lightning damage (choose one) for the spell‘s duration.
(Rank 3) The target suffers a weakness to blast, fire, and lightning damage for the spell‘s duration.

name: Earthen Tremor
effect:
You send shockwaves through the earth, causing the ground to tremble violently. Target all creatures in the spell’s area.
Weak. Deal +2 damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +4 damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +6 damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to medium.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +6/+12/+18 damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Electrified Weapon
effect:
Imbue a weapon with the power of storms. If the target is dual-wielding, you can instead target both their held weapons.
On a success, the weapon deals +2 lightning damage. On a strong or critical hit, the attack‘s target is briefly staggered. Each creature can suffer the condition from this spell only once per scene. If you target your own weapon, you can make a weapon attack with it as part of casting this spell.
This spell lasts for a medium duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) The weapon deals +4 lightning damage instead.
(Rank 3) The weapon deals +6 lightning damage instead.

name: Lightning Javelin
effect:
You conjure pure lightning in the form of a spear and hurl it against a creature.
Weak. Deal +4 lightning damage.
Strong. Deal +8 lightning damage and the target is briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +12 lightning damage and the target is briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 lightning damage instead.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 lightning damage instead.

name: Storm Coat
effect:
You weave a protective coat of elemental energies around a creature.
On a success, the creature gains the following effects:
- You gain resistance against blast, frost, and lightning damage.
- Whenever you are hit by a melee attack, the attacker takes +0 lightning damage (ignore AV).
heightened:
(Rank 2) The attacker takes +2 lightning damage (ignore AV) instead.
(Rank 3) The attacker takes +4 lightning damage (ignore AV) instead.

name: Storm Shield
effect:
You quickly conjure a personal barrier of swirling air. Cast this spell when you are attacked vs. your Parry or Dodge.
On a success, you can defend with your Resist instead of Parry or Dodge until your next turn. When a melee attack against you fails, the attacker takes +0 blast damage (ignore AV). If a ranged attack against you fails, you can divert the attack towards another creature in close range. If the ranged attack blunders, you can even divert it back to the attacker. When diverting an attack to another creature, it automatically hits with a weak success.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Failed melee attacks inflict +2 blast damage (ignore AV) instead. Diverted ranged attacks deal +2 damage.
(Rank 3) Failed melee attacks inflict +4 blast damage (ignore AV) instead. Diverted ranged attacks deal +4 damage.

name: Volcanic Bolt
effect:
You magically dissolve some earth or rock in your surroundings into lava. You can hurl the lava as a searing projectile against a creature.
Weak. Deal +4 fire damage.
Strong. Deal +8 fire damage and they suffer burning (2) for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +12 fire damage and they suffer burning (4) for a short duration.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Deal +6/+12/+18 fire damage instead and inflict burning (4/6) on a strong or critical success.
(Rank 3) Deal +8/+16/+24 fire damage instead and inflict burning (6/8) on a strong or critical success.

name: Wind Hose
effect:
You create a powerful swirl of air around the target location, pulling smaller, loose objects into it’s vortex.
Weak. Deal +3 damage.
Strong. Deal +6 damage and if the target is medium or smaller in size, they are also grappled by the wind hose.
Critical. Deal +9 damage and if the target is medium or smaller in size, they are also grappled and restrained by the wind hose.
When you successfully cast this spell against a target and it is grappled by the wind hose, you gain +1 boon when casting the spell again against the same target on your next turn.
heightened:
(Rank 2) Choose one:
- Deal +5/+10/+15 damage instead.
- You can also target large creatures for grappling.
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +7/+14/+21 damage instead.
- You can also target huge creatures for grappling.

name: Conjure Elemental
effect:
You conjure a being of pure elemental power. Choose either air, earth, fire, or water.
On a success, an elemental creature of the chosen element appears at any point within range.
Treat the elemental as your companion (see Mounts & Companions for more). It is friendly towards you and your allies while you maintain concentration for this spell.
The elemental has the statistics of a tier 2 companion. In addition, it gains a companion trait, according to which element you have chosen.
You can dismiss the elemental as a Quick Action during your turn. Otherwise it remains until reduced to 0 HP or for a short duration.
If you lose concentration on this spell, the elemental becomes hostile to you and your allies. Roll initiative for it and treat it as an enemy. You can’t dismiss the elemental anymore and it remains until reduced to 0 HP or after the spell’s duration.
heightened: (Rank 3) You conjure an elemental with the statistics of a tier 3 companion instead.

name: Lightning Bolt
effect:
You summon a powerful bolt of lightning that streaks forth in a straight line from you to a target point up to a short distance away, electrifying every creature it passes.
Weak. Deal +3 lightning damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
Critical. Deal +9 lightning damage to each target and they are briefly staggered.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 lightning damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to long.

name: Lightning Step
effect:
In a sudden flash of lightning you move at the speed of sound to another place. Cast this spell on your turn.
Choose any point within range you can see, where you would be able to move to by walking, climbing, swimming, or jumping.
On a success, you instantly move to the chosen location (this isn’t teleportation) without provoking Opportunity Attacks. When you arrive, you produce a loud booming sound, dealing +2 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) to all creatures in close range of you. Your Movement for the rest of the turn becomes 0.
heightened: (Rank 3) Increase the spell’s range to long and deal +4 blast damage (ignore 1/2 AV) instead.

name: Magma Burst
effect:
You channel the earth’s wrath, making the target location’s ground burst with lava. Target all creatures in melee range of it.
Weak. Deal +4 fire damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +8 fire damage to each target and they suffer burning (2) for a short duration.
Critical. Deal +12 fire damage to each target and they suffer burning (4) for a short duration.
The target location remains covered with cracks filled with lava for a short duration, after which it cools down and remains as solid rock.
Each creature ending their turn in melee range of it automatically suffer the effects of a weak hit.
If any creature has direct contact with the lava, they instead automatically suffer the effects of a critical hit.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +6/+12/+18 fire damage to each target and inflict burning (4/6) on a strong or critical success instead.
- Target all creatures in close range of the target location instead.

name: Pyroclasm
effect:
You slam the earth before you and it erupts with force, spitting flaming hot lava into the air.
Weak. Deal +3 fire damage to each target.
Strong. Deal +6 fire damage to each target and they fall prone.
Critical. Deal +9 fire damage to each target, they fall prone, and their Movement briefly becomes 0.
The area affected by this spell remains as broken ground and is considered difficult terrain.
heightened:
(Rank 3) Choose one:
- Deal +4/+8/+12 fire damage to each target instead.
- Increase the spell‘s range to medium.

name: Storm Cloud
effect:
You summon a dark cloud brewing with electricity. On a success, the cloud extends in close range of the target location and hovers up to a medium distance above it. Target up to three creatures below the cloud use your roll’s result vs. their Dodge.
Weak. Deal +4 lightning damage against each target.
